problem_summary: The diary Skill has accumulated all capture, thought, decision, weekly, governance, and revision instructions in a 20,695-byte SKILL.md and requires the 9,024-byte agent protocol for every capture. create-draft eagerly retrieves and hydrates historical entries and goal context, returns roughly 14-16 KB for ordinary examples, repeats initialization, and load_json can interpret long inline JSON as a path. Historical short captures consequently use unnecessary model context and tool retries.
impact: Ordinary diary preview turns have taken roughly 63-77 seconds and 6-7 tool calls even though the local deterministic create-draft work is about 43 ms. The workflow becomes slower as confirmed entries grow, wastes model context, and makes simple capture feel disproportionate to the task.
proposed_diff: 1. Reduce the main SKILL.md to at most 8 KB with only triggers, preservation and confirmation invariants, entry-type rules, and a routing table. Split ordinary capture, thought feedback, decision analysis, weekly review, theme/goal governance, and Skill improvement into directly linked one-level reference files; remove the unconditional agent-protocol read. 2. Make ordinary diary/thought capture speed-first: no historical text, goal interpretation, or diary feedback by default; thought feedback is a separate non-authoritative comment of at most 120 Chinese characters; decision and weekly retain full analysis; explicit deep requests and strong deterministic signals load bounded context. 3. Add a read-only project custom agent using gpt-5.6-terra with model_reasoning_effort=low. Use at most one worker only for complex speech, multi-topic input, or uncertain terms; keep ordinary capture in the parent and decision/weekly on the parent model. Prevent that worker from loading the full diary Skill and give it a compact cleaning/classification JSON contract. 4. Add create-draft --analysis-mode auto|deep|none, default auto. Auto returns empty historical and goal context for ordinary diary/thought, compact continuity evidence for strong continuity signals, and decision context for explicit decision type. Preserve legacy response keys with empty values where applicable. 5. Add capture-context --entry-id --profile continuity|thought|decision|goals|deep so an entry reclassified after draft can fetch only the required context without creating a second draft. 6. Return compact active theme_candidates selected deterministically from names, aliases, and tags without returning source journal text. Stop by serialized byte budget rather than a fixed record count. 7. Refactor retrieval to score lightweight entry fields first and hydrate segments, decisions, and feedback only for selected rows; make internal calls share one initialization/connection; count full serialized JSON against context budgets. Target ordinary create-draft output at no more than 4 KB on the current representative fixture. 8. Fix CLI load_json so strings beginning with { or [ are parsed directly and other values are treated as paths with OSError-safe fallback; keep old CLI arguments compatible. 9. Add tests and temporary-copy performance measurements for ordinary diary, complex speech, thought, explicit and reclassified decision, strong continuity, deep context, weekly behavior, long inline JSON, and legacy interfaces.
compatibility: Preserve the local-only standard-library architecture, SQLite schema truth, verbatim originals, draft-preview-explicit-confirm boundary, existing save-preview/search/confirm interfaces, decision structure, weekly review semantics, theme and goal approval gates, and Markdown exports. Existing callers continue to receive context and goal_context keys, but ordinary auto mode returns compact empty values unless routing requires context. No OpenAI API client, API key, external model call, automatic parent-model change, or Fast mode is introduced.
tests: Run python3 -m py_compile for diary_agent and the journal wrapper.
Run /mnt/d/Project_PA/condaenv_host/bin/python -m pytest for the complete repository suite.
Run git diff --check plus SQLite PRAGMA integrity_check and foreign_key_check.
Assert a short clear diary uses empty historical/goal context, no worker route, and a create-draft response no larger than 4 KB on a representative temporary database copy.
Assert continuity, deep, thought, decision, goals, weekly, long inline JSON, and legacy CLI paths remain correct.
Forward-test the Terra low custom worker against a temporary repository/database copy and verify at most one worker is used only for complex cleaning/classification.
token_cost_change: decrease: the always-loaded Skill plus capture protocol is targeted to fall from about 29.7 KB to at most about 12 KB for ordinary capture, while ordinary create-draft output falls from about 14-16 KB to at most 4 KB and simple captures avoid subagent fan-out.
